title: File not writable error in Read/Write Files node
symptom: When attempting to write a file using the Read/Write Files from Disk node, n8n throws an error stating that the file is not writable.
applies_when: Using Read/Write Files from Disk node
not_this_if: File path is correct and accessible
Running n8n on a different operating system
root_cause: The error occurs because n8n restricts file access to a default directory, which may not include the specified file path. If the environment variable for file access is not set correctly, n8n cannot write to the intended location.
fix_steps: Set the environment variable N8N_RESTRICT_FILE_ACCESS_TO to the desired writable directory path.
Restart n8n in a new PowerShell window to apply the changes.
agent_instruction: Set the N8N_RESTRICT_FILE_ACCESS_TO environment variable to your desired writable directory and restart n8n.
